channel.title: Governance Under Question
channel.description: Independent updates on AI governance, security, bias, and reports/benchmarks. Companion publication for original research papers.
channel.copyright: © 2026 Governance Under Question. CC BY 4.0 unless otherwise noted.

item.title: Working Paper: Signed Multi-Layer Event Tokens for AI Governance
item.description: Three-layer signature construction (per-layer HMAC + coordinator signature + outer signature) that makes multimodal AI events tamper-evident in O(1) verification. Reference implementation, ~1,400-event ledger, and constitutional overlay open-source. Working paper v0.1 — PDF + arXiv submission targeted for Q3 2026.

item.title: Issue 001: Why AI Governance Needs Evidence, Not Vibes
item.description: Opening report. Defines evidence-first governance as four requirements: a specific measurable claim, a reproducible eval, a tamper-evident artifact, and stated limits. Sets the standard every future issue and paper will be audited against.
